title: Sonar Vendor Selection Meeting — 2024-08-29
text:
# Sonar Vendor Selection Meeting — 2024-08-29

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Supplier Selection Review  
**Attendees:** Cass Doyle (Payload), Felix Herrera (Integration), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE), Nina Schulz (Quality)  
**Chair:** Cass Doyle  
**Minutes recorded by:** Nina Schulz

---

## Background

The original sonar transducer supplier (DepthWave Systems) was selected at programme start in 2023 and supplied the initial P-1148 specification. During the first sonar integration test series (KES-156), the DepthWave unit exhibited unacceptable frequency drift: the centre frequency shifted from the specified 200kHz to 184kHz over a 2-hour thermal soak at 35°C. This was documented in KES-158 (frequency drift bug). The sonar signal processor (P-1147) and its firmware were tuned to a 200kHz centre frequency and cannot compensate for a 16kHz drift without firmware rework and re-qualification.

The engineering team evaluated two alternative suppliers: AquaSound Corp and MarineSpec Ltd.

## Supplier Evaluation Summary

| Criterion | DepthWave (original) | AquaSound Corp | MarineSpec Ltd |
|---|---|---|---|
| Centre frequency stability | ±16kHz over temp (FAIL) | ±1.5kHz over temp | ±3kHz over temp |
| Broadband range | 100–300kHz | 100–400kHz | 120–350kHz |
| Depth rating | 20m | 30m | 25m |
| Lead time | 6 weeks | 10 weeks | 8 weeks |
| IP68 connector | No | Yes | Yes |
| Price per unit | $2,100 AUD | $3,400 AUD | $2,900 AUD |
| Existing qualification data | None | ITAR-free, DNV-GL cert | None |

## Discussion

Cass Doyle recommended AquaSound Corp. The broader frequency range (100–400kHz) is a bonus for future multi-frequency operation. The DNV-GL certification significantly reduces the qualification test burden. The 10-week lead time is manageable if ECR-218 is raised immediately.

Dr. Voss approved the selection subject to Cass Doyle confirming the O-ring flange dimensions match P-1149 mounting bracket (DRW-517) — confirmed in the meeting.

Nina Schulz noted that the sonar mounting bracket (P-1149, DRW-517) and the sonar ICD (DOC-462) will both need updating for the AquaSound interface.

## Decision

**AGREED:** Change sonar transducer from DepthWave to AquaSound Corp (P-1148 Rev B).

- Decision maker: Cass Doyle (payload lead), approved by Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE)
- Rationale: DepthWave frequency drift (KES-158) cannot be tolerated; AquaSound has superior stability, broader bandwidth, and DNV-GL qualification data
- ECR to be raised: ECR-218 (Cass Doyle)
- Documents to update: DOC-462 (Sonar ICD), DRW-517 (mounting drawing — O-ring interface check complete)
- Firmware: SRC-011 sonar_driver.c DSP filter tuning to be updated by Cass Doyle

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-218 for sonar vendor change | Cass Doyle | 2024-09-05 |
| A2 | Order 3 AquaSound P-1148 units for qualification | Cass Doyle | 2024-09-10 |
| A3 | Update DOC-462 Sonar ICD for AquaSound interface | Cass Doyle | 2024-10-15 |
| A4 | Update SRC-011 DSP filter for AquaSound frequency response | Cass Doyle | 2024-10-30 |
| A5 | Update DRW-517 drawing notes (confirmed compatible) | Tobias Wren | 2024-09-20 |
